+# Execution Report
+
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-140
+AUTHOR: Executor / Claude
+TIMESTAMP: 2026-05-24_04-15-05+0300
+RELATED_FILES:
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-140_EXEC_2026-05-24_04-15-05+0300.md — this read-only non-time-scale CommandBus preflight report (the only file the Executor created).
+STATUS: NEEDS_HUMAN
+EXEC_STATUS: blocked
+
+## Task Packet Reference
+
+`ai_handoffs/ISSUE-140_TASK_2026-05-24_04-06-15+0300.md`
+
+## Pre-Execution Review Consumed
+
+No pre-execution review issued for this dispatch.
+
+## What I Changed
+
+### Source
+
+- None. This is a read-only audit per the TASK packet's `MAY edit: None.` and
+  the Acceptance Criteria's "read-only except for its own generated EXEC
+  report, optional matching sidecar, and possible queue-runner log" rule. No
+  `crates/**`, `editor/**`, `kernel/**`, `.github/**`, Cargo, PowerShell,
+  shell script, schema, architecture lint, doctrine, status, planning, ADR,
+  fixture, generated asset, or root-level repository document was edited or
+  created.
+
+### Tests
+
+- None added. No test file was edited.
+
+### Docs
+
+- None added outside this EXEC report.
+
+## Per-File Summary
+
+Single new file: this EXEC report. The matching `.meta.json` sidecar is
+created by `new-handoff.ps1 -Finalize` if/when the orchestrator finalizes
+the packet. No other tracked or untracked changes were introduced by the
+Executor in this dispatch.
+
+## Verification Results
+
+This dispatch is read-only. No `cargo`, formatter, architecture-lint,
+`cargo-deny`, doctest, bench, source-generation, doc-generation, or
+`.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` command was run, per the TASK packet's
+`Verification Gates` ("No cargo, formatter, architecture-lint, or test
+command is required as an Executor gate for this read-only audit").
+
+### Verification gates actually run
+
+1. `git status --short --untracked-files=no` (pre-audit) — exit 0, empty
+   output. No pre-existing tracked changes. Documents the clean baseline
+   per the TASK packet's first Verification Gate.
+2. `git log -1 --pretty='%h %s'` — `05a442e chore(dispatch): seed task #28
+   non-time-scale CommandBus preflight`. Branch baseline.
+3. `git status --short --untracked-files=no` (post-EXEC) — exit 0; expected
+   output empty (the EXEC report itself is an untracked file, not a
+   tracked modification, and so does not appear here). The
+   `--untracked-files=no` flag is explicit in the TASK packet's
+   Verification Gates; running `git status` without that flag would
+   additionally list this dispatch's own allowed untracked artifacts
+   (`ai_handoffs/ISSUE-140_TASK_*.{md,meta.json}` inbound, plus this
+   EXEC `.md` and its eventual `.meta.json` sidecar). No tracked source,
+   test, Cargo, workflow, script, doctrine, status-doc, existing-packet,
+   or issue/label metadata file changed.
+
+### Read-only inspection log
+
+The audit relied entirely on the Claude agent harness `Read` / `Grep` /
+`Glob` tools (filesystem reads, no shell `rg` / `grep` invocations) plus
+the two `git status --short` plus `git log -1` shell commands above. The
+relevant inspected paths:
+
+- `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-140_TASK_2026-05-24_04-06-15+0300.md` (full).
+- `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/commands.rs` (full).
+- `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/playback.rs` (full).
+- `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/asset_reload.rs` (full).
+- `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs` (header doc + struct +
+  builders 1–950, accessors + handlers + helpers 950–1300,
+  `ApplicationHandler::window_event` 1500–1744).
+- `crates/editor-shell/src/pick_path.rs` (full).
+- `crates/editor-shell/src/world.rs` (full).
+- `crates/editor-shell/src/coord.rs` (full).
+- `crates/editor-actions/src/lib.rs`, `src/action.rs`, `src/bus.rs` head
+  (sufficient to confirm `Action: Send + Sync + 'static` with
+  `apply(&self, &mut World)` / `revert(&self, &mut World)` and bus
+  signature `submit(Box<dyn Action>, &mut World)`).
+- `crates/editor-state/src/lib.rs`, `src/selection.rs`. The other
+  `editor-state` modules (`hover`, `active_tool`, `face_selection`,
+  `modal_state`, `drag_drop`, `inspector_snapshot`) were enumerated via
+  Glob.
+- `crates/editor-shell/tests/*.rs` enumerated via Glob (no test was edited).
+- Prior EXEC precedents read for evidence (not authority):
+  `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-108_EXEC_2026-05-23_10-08-36+0300.md` (full),
+  `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-132_EXEC_2026-05-23_23-01-08+0300.md` (selected
+  Q2/Q3 portions), `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-134_EXEC_2026-05-23_23-40-55+0300.md`
+  (full), `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-138_EXEC_2026-05-24_02-49-15+0300.md`
+  (full).
+
+### Halt-condition outcomes
+
+- "Answering Q1-Q5 requires editing source / tests / Cargo / …" — PASS
+  (no forbidden file was edited; Q1-Q5 answered entirely from reading).
+- "The current code cannot be inspected enough to provide file/line
+  evidence" — PASS (file/line evidence present for every candidate
+  classification).
+- "The only possible recommendation requires changing the `Action`
+  trait, `CommandBus`, `CompoundAction`, `EditorShell::submit_action`,
+  or any CommandBus signature" — TRIGGERED. There is no non-time-scale
+  user action under the current `Action::apply(&mut rge_kernel_ecs::World)`
+  contract today. Per Acceptance Criteria, this triggers the
+  `NEEDS_HUMAN` route (Q5), not a forbidden source edit. The Executor
+  did NOT change any of those surfaces.
+- "Smallest apparent candidate requires editor-state mutation,
+  editor-shell field mutation, render/gfx mutation, CAD wrapper-world
+  mutation, asset reload side effects, playback state mutation, UI /
+  menu / toolbar / egui wiring, or broader editor context that does
+  not fit the current World-only `Action` trait" — TRIGGERED for every
+  non-time-scale candidate inventoried below. See Q2 / Q3.
+- "Answer tries to pick `SetTimeScale`, another time-scale preset,
+  step-up/step-down binding, preset trio work, UI/menu/toolbar/egui
+  wiring, or any other time-scale follow-up as task #29" — NOT
+  triggered. Q5 routes to `NEEDS_HUMAN`, not to a time-scale follow-up.
+- "Prior CommandBus EXEC packet evidence … missing, unreadable, or
+  contradictory" — PASS (ISSUE-108, ISSUE-132, ISSUE-134, ISSUE-138
+  EXEC packets present and consistent with current code).
+- "Required verification command shows tracked changes outside this
+  dispatch's own allowed handoff/log artifacts" — PASS (pre and post
+  `git status --short --untracked-files=no` both empty).
+- "Any forbidden edit or forbidden artifact is created" — PASS (none
+  created).
+- "Correction prompt asks the Executor to fix any source/test/Cargo/…
+  failure for this read-only audit" — NOT triggered (no correction
+  round invoked; this is the first EXEC for the dispatch).
+
+## 5-Question Non-Time-Scale CommandBus Preflight Answer Block
+
+### Q1. What non-time-scale editor user actions exist today, and where are they handled?
+
+Inventory of every editor-shell user-input branch / user-visible handler
+that is **not** the `SetTimeScale` action (excluded by scope), with file
+and line evidence. The scope is "obvious editor-shell user actions"
+including keyboard, toolbar, reload, selection, playback, and tool axes
+per the TASK Deliverables Q1 bullet.
+
+**Keyboard — Ctrl-modified (already CommandBus-bound, navigation only).**
+
+- `Ctrl+Z` → `EditorKeyCommand::Undo` → `EditorShell::undo_command` →
+  `CommandBus::undo(&mut World)`. Definitions:
+  `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/commands.rs:67` (variant),
+  `:127` (`KeyCode::KeyZ` arm in `from_key_press`),
+  `:332-339` (handler arm in `handle_key_command`),
+  `:290-292` (`undo_command` wrapper). Dispatched from
+  `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs:1704-1705`
+  (`if let Some(cmd) = EditorKeyCommand::from_key_press(key, ctrl,
+  shift) { self.handle_key_command(cmd); }`).
+- `Ctrl+Y` → `EditorKeyCommand::Redo` → `EditorShell::redo_command` →
+  `CommandBus::redo(&mut World)`. Same files,
+  `commands.rs:69` / `:128` / `:340-347` / `:301-303`.
+- `Ctrl+S` → `EditorKeyCommand::MarkSaved` → `EditorShell::mark_saved_command`
+  → `CommandBus::mark_saved()`. `commands.rs:72` / `:129` / `:348` /
+  `:308-310`.
+
+These three are bus navigation (cursor / save-mark movement), not new
+mutating `submit` actions, and the TASK packet's scope is "non-time-scale
+user action that could become the next `Action` impl through
+`CommandBus::submit`". They are listed here for completeness so Q2
+classification covers every keyboard binding observed in `window_event`.
+
+**Keyboard — Ctrl+digit (time-scale; explicitly excluded from candidate
+set per TASK scope).**
+
+- `Ctrl+2` → `EditorKeyCommand::SetTimeScaleDoubleSpeed` →
+  `self.set_time_scale(2.0)` → `SetTimeScale`. `commands.rs:74-78` /
+  `:130` / `:349`.
+- `Ctrl+0` → `EditorKeyCommand::ResetTimeScaleDefault` →
+  `self.set_time_scale(TimeScale::DEFAULT)`. `commands.rs:79-85` /
+  `:131` / `:350`.
+- `Ctrl+4` → `EditorKeyCommand::SetTimeScaleMaxFastForward` →
+  `self.set_time_scale(TimeScale::MAX)`. `commands.rs:86-92` / `:132` /
+  `:351`.
+
+Listed here ONLY to anchor the exclusion. Per TASK Acceptance Criteria
+and Halt Conditions, **none** of these (nor any other time-scale
+follow-up — presets, step-up/step-down, preset trios, UI/menu/toolbar/
+egui wiring) is eligible for the recommended task #29.
+
+**Keyboard — plain (no modifiers).**
+
+- `Space` → `EditorPlaybackCommand::TogglePlay` →
+  `EditorShell::handle_playback_command(TogglePlay)` →
+  `self.handle_button(ToolbarButtonId::Play | Pause)`. Definitions:
+  `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/playback.rs:88-92` (variant),
+  `:119-120` (Space arm in `from_key_press`),
+  `:156-180` (handler dispatching to `handle_button`),
+  `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs:1126-1180`
+  (`handle_button` body — Play/Pause/Stop/Step/FrameStep).
+  Dispatched from `lifecycle/mod.rs:1706-1709`.
+- `Escape` → `EditorPlaybackCommand::Stop` →
+  `EditorShell::handle_playback_command(Stop)` →
+  `self.handle_button(ToolbarButtonId::Stop)`. `playback.rs:94-98` /
+  `:121` / `:181-197`.
+- `R` (no modifiers) → `EditorShell::handle_asset_reload()`.
+  Dispatched from `lifecycle/mod.rs:1710-1721`
+  (`else if key == KeyCode::KeyR && self.modifiers.is_empty() {
+  self.handle_asset_reload(); }`); handler body in
+  `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/asset_reload.rs:118-178`.
+
+**Mouse.**
+
+- `WindowEvent::MouseInput { state: Pressed, button: Left, .. }` →
+  `EditorShell::handle_left_click()`. Dispatch site:
+  `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs:1641-1666`; handler body:
+  `crates/editor-shell/src/pick_path.rs:58-121`. The handler also
+  invokes `EditorShell::rebuild_highlight_overlay()`
+  (`pick_path.rs:134-204`).
+- `WindowEvent::CursorMoved { position, .. }` → updates
+  `self.cursor_pos`. `lifecycle/mod.rs:1634-1640`. Bookkeeping, no
+  user-visible mutation, no undo semantics — listed because it is a
+  user-input event observed by the shell.
+
+**Window / lifecycle events.**
+
+- `WindowEvent::CloseRequested` → `event_loop.exit()`.
+  `lifecycle/mod.rs:1555-1562`. Lifecycle termination; not a user
+  *content* action.
+- `WindowEvent::Resized(new_size)` → `viewport.resize` +
+  `EditorShell::resize_render_path(&render_input, w, h)` +
+  `EguiHost::resize(w, h, scale)`. `lifecycle/mod.rs:1563-1610`.
+- `WindowEvent::RedrawRequested` → `tick_redraw()` + `render_frame()`.
+  `lifecycle/mod.rs:1611-1633`. Per-frame driver, not a discrete user
+  action.
+- `WindowEvent::ModifiersChanged(new_modifiers)` → updates
+  `self.modifiers`. `lifecycle/mod.rs:1668-1675`. Bookkeeping.
+
+**Toolbar buttons (directly invocable; not currently bound to a
+production UI but reachable via the public `handle_button` API).**
+
+- `ToolbarButtonId::Play` → captures `WorldSnapshot` + sets `PlayState`
+  + records `AuditEvent::PlayPressed`. `lifecycle/mod.rs:1131-1143`.
+- `ToolbarButtonId::Pause` → `PlayState::pause()` + records
+  `AuditEvent::PausePressed`. `lifecycle/mod.rs:1144-1148`.
+- `ToolbarButtonId::Stop` → restores `WorldSnapshot` + records
+  `AuditEvent::StopPressed`. `lifecycle/mod.rs:1149-1160`.
+- `ToolbarButtonId::Step` → advances one game tick + records
+  `AuditEvent::StepPressed`. `lifecycle/mod.rs:1161-1169`.
+- `ToolbarButtonId::FrameStep` → advances one frame's worth of ticks
+  + records `AuditEvent::FrameStepPressed`. `lifecycle/mod.rs:1170-1178`.
+
+**Tool / selection / coordination mutations.**
+
+- `EditorShell::coord_mut()` exposes mutable
+  `EditorCoord { selection, active_tool, face_selection }`.
+  `lifecycle/mod.rs:1011-1013`. The single production writer to
+  `coord.face_selection` is `pick_path.rs:94-97`
+  (`self.coord.face_selection.clear(); … add(sel)`); the other coord
+  fields (`selection`, `active_tool`) have **no production writer in
+  the editor-shell branches** — they are written only from test code
+  and external callers via `coord_mut()`. `crates/editor-state/src/lib.rs`
+  re-exports `ActiveTool`, `Hover`, `FaceSelection`,
+  `FaceSelectionSet`, `Selection`. The `EditorCoord` struct itself
+  lives at `crates/editor-shell/src/coord.rs:18-33`.
+
+**Render-only mutations (not user actions per se, listed for Q2
+completeness).**
+
+- `EditorShell::reload_render_assets(meshes, base_colors, textures)` —
+  defined in `crates/editor-shell/src/render_path.rs` (consumed by
+  `handle_asset_reload`). Not directly user-invoked; reached only via
+  the `R` keypress above.
+
+### Q2. Which candidates are genuinely World-only under the current Action trait?
+
+Classifications follow the exact taxonomy in the TASK packet
+(`world-only`, `editor-shell-field`, `editor-state`,
+`cad/editor-wrapper-world`, `render/gfx`, `needs-new-context`) and are
+tied to current file/line evidence — not to desired architecture or
+file names. The `world-only` definition matches the current `Action`
+trait verbatim: a candidate qualifies only if its `apply` / `revert`
+can be expressed as a mutation to **`&mut rge_kernel_ecs::World`**
+delivered by `EditorShell::submit_action` (which routes through
+`self.command_bus.submit(action, self.world.kernel_mut())` —
+`commands.rs:280`). The editor wrapper world's kernel (`self.world.kernel`,
+defined at `crates/editor-shell/src/world.rs:57`) holds **one**
+production resource today: `TimeScale`
+(installed at `lifecycle/mod.rs:576, 661, 902` via
+`world.kernel_mut().insert_resource(TimeScale::default())`). The
+**separate** `cad_world: Option<KernelWorld>` field
+(`lifecycle/mod.rs:301`) is the CAD-domain kernel world; it is NOT
+the world the bus mutates — the bus never sees it.
+
+- `Ctrl+Z` / `Ctrl+Y` / `Ctrl+S` — **not a candidate** (bus navigation
+  only). The undo/redo cursor moves and `mark_saved` flag update apply
+  `Action::revert` / `Action::apply` of entries already on the bus
+  stack; they do not submit new actions. Acknowledged by the TASK
+  Halt Conditions explicitly excluding these via the Q3 framing
+  ("smallest non-time-scale World-only candidate"). Evidence:
+  `commands.rs:290-310`, `lifecycle/mod.rs:1700-1705`.
+
+- `Ctrl+2` / `Ctrl+0` / `Ctrl+4` — **excluded from candidate set by
+  scope.** Even though their mutation envelope is `world-only`
+  (`world.insert_resource(TimeScale::with_value(...))` —
+  `commands.rs:237, 242`), the TASK packet's Acceptance Criteria and
+  Halt Conditions disqualify "`SetTimeScale`, time-scale presets,
+  step-up/step-down bindings, preset trio work, UI/menu/toolbar/egui
+  wiring, and any other time-scale follow-up" from being task #29.
+
+- `Space` / `Escape` (playback) — **`editor-shell-field`**.
+  `handle_playback_command` (`playback.rs:156-198`) routes to
+  `handle_button` (`lifecycle/mod.rs:1126-1180`), which mutates:
+  `self.state: PlayState` (`lifecycle/mod.rs:249`),
+  `self.snapshot: Option<WorldSnapshot>` (`lifecycle/mod.rs:250`),
+  `self.audit: AuditLedger` (`lifecycle/mod.rs:262`), and
+  `self.tick_count: u64` (`lifecycle/mod.rs:265`). None of those are
+  `rge_kernel_ecs::World` resources or components — they are direct
+  fields of the editor shell. Reinforced by
+  `playback.rs:6` ("No CommandBus involvement; no new ECS state; no
+  toolbar UI") as the intentional design.
+
+- `R` (asset hot-reload) — **`render/gfx`** plus **`editor-shell-field`**.
+  `handle_asset_reload` (`asset_reload.rs:118-178`) reads
+  `self.glb_source_path: Option<PathBuf>` (`lifecycle/mod.rs:379`),
+  invokes `self.reload_hook` (`lifecycle/mod.rs:387`), and passes
+  the result to `self.reload_render_assets(...)` which swaps GPU mesh /
+  material / texture vectors held on `EditorShell` (`self.meshes`,
+  `self.materials`, `self.prebuilt_render_*` —
+  `lifecycle/mod.rs:417, 439, 338, 351, 371`). Pure render/gfx + I/O
+  effect; no `&mut rge_kernel_ecs::World` mutation at all. Reinforced
+  by `asset_reload.rs:6-9` ("Companion to commands.rs (CommandBus-bound)
+  and playback.rs (state-machine-bound). This file holds the third
+  keyboard axis: plain `R` → re-import …").
+
+- Mouse left-click face pick — **`editor-state`** + **`render/gfx`**
+  (with reads from **`cad/editor-wrapper-world`**, not mutations to it).
+  `handle_left_click` (`pick_path.rs:58-121`) mutates
+  `self.coord.face_selection: editor_state::FaceSelectionSet`
+  (`pick_path.rs:94, 97` via `clear()`/`add(sel)`, declared at
+  `crates/editor-shell/src/coord.rs:32`) — that is `editor-state`,
+  not `World`. It then calls `rebuild_highlight_overlay`
+  (`pick_path.rs:134-204`) which mutates
+  `self.highlight_index_buffer: Option<IndexBuffer>`
+  (`lifecycle/mod.rs:470`, `pick_path.rs:194`) — that is `render/gfx`.
+  The handler reads `self.cad_world: Option<KernelWorld>`
+  (`lifecycle/mod.rs:301`) and `self.cad_graph: Option<CadGraph>`
+  (`lifecycle/mod.rs:311`) but does NOT mutate either. Crucially,
+  `cad_world` is the **CAD-domain** kernel world, NOT the same
+  `rge_kernel_ecs::World` the bus mutates — the bus mutates
+  `self.world.kernel` (`commands.rs:280`,
+  `world.rs:57`), a separate `rge_kernel_ecs::World` instance.
+
+- `WindowEvent::CursorMoved` — **`editor-shell-field`** (bookkeeping).
+  Mutates `self.cursor_pos: Option<[f32; 2]>` (`lifecycle/mod.rs:446,
+  1639`). Not user-visible, no undo semantics.
+
+- `WindowEvent::ModifiersChanged` — **`editor-shell-field`** (bookkeeping).
+  Mutates `self.modifiers: ModifiersState` (`lifecycle/mod.rs:514,
+  1674`). Not user-visible content; supports the keyboard handler.
+
+- `WindowEvent::Resized` — **`editor-shell-field`** plus **`render/gfx`**.
+  Mutates `self.viewport: Viewport` (`lifecycle/mod.rs:261, 1564`),
+  rebuilds the wgpu surface configuration via
+  `self.resize_render_path(...)` (touches `self.surface_ctx`,
+  `self.compiled_frame_graph`, etc. on `lifecycle/mod.rs:397, 490`,
+  rebuilt in `render_path.rs`), and forwards to `EguiHost::resize(...)`
+  (mutates `self.egui_host: Option<EguiHost>`, `lifecycle/mod.rs:533`).
+  No `&mut rge_kernel_ecs::World` mutation.
+
+- `WindowEvent::CloseRequested` — **`editor-shell-field`** (lifecycle
+  termination only — `event_loop.exit()`,
+  `lifecycle/mod.rs:1555-1562`).
+
+- `WindowEvent::RedrawRequested` — **`editor-shell-field`** + tick driver.
+  Calls `self.tick_redraw()` (advances `self.tick_count`, publishes a
+  `RenderInputOwned` to the handoff slot, updates the viewport
+  overlay — `lifecycle/mod.rs:1196-1247`) and then `self.render_frame()`.
+  Not a discrete user action; not a candidate.
+
+- Toolbar `handle_button(Play | Pause | Stop | Step | FrameStep)` —
+  **`editor-shell-field`**. Mutates `self.state`, `self.snapshot`,
+  `self.audit`, `self.tick_count` (`lifecycle/mod.rs:1131-1178`). Same
+  surface as Space/Escape; reinforces that playback is intentionally
+  bus-free.
+
+- `EditorCoord::selection` mutation (no production writer today;
+  `Selection: BTreeSet<EntityId>` per
+  `crates/editor-state/src/selection.rs:58-60`) — **`editor-state`**.
+  `Selection` is owned at `self.coord.selection`
+  (`crates/editor-shell/src/coord.rs:21`), reached only via
+  `coord_mut()` (`lifecycle/mod.rs:1011`). The TASK packet explicitly
+  flags `editor-state` as a non-eligible classification, and the
+  `crates/editor-state` module-level doctrine (`src/lib.rs:11-14`,
+  `:18-19`) states "Coordination state, NOT authoritative content.
+  Stores `EntityId` references and primitive coordination context —
+  never component bodies …" and "Five categories — fixed at v0.8 per
+  architecture freeze (§0.6)." The `editor-state-ownership`
+  architecture lint enforces this (per ISSUE-108 EXEC §Q3 and the
+  `editor-state/src/lib.rs:12` doc comment).
+
+- `EditorCoord::active_tool` mutation (no production writer today;
+  `ActiveTool` lives at `crates/editor-state/src/active_tool.rs`) —
+  **`editor-state`**. Same classification reason as `selection`.
+
+- `EditorCoord::face_selection` mutation (the **only** production
+  coord writer is `pick_path.rs:94-97`) — **`editor-state`**, already
+  covered under the mouse left-click row above.
+
+- `EditorCoord::hover` / `EditorCoord::modal_state` /
+  `EditorCoord::drag_drop` — **not exposed on `EditorCoord` today**.
+  `coord.rs:18-33` documents that only `selection`, `active_tool`,
+  and `face_selection` are wired up; the other three "join when their
+  owning waves land (W08+ editor-ui)". No current production user
+  path mutates them; therefore **not a candidate**.
+
+- `CadGraph::begin_operation` / `commit` / `rollback` / `restore_to`
+  (CAD operator history) — **`cad/editor-wrapper-world`**. The
+  `self.cad_graph: Option<CadGraph>` field (`lifecycle/mod.rs:311`)
+  is **not** an `rge_kernel_ecs::World`; it is a separate type from
+  `rge_cad_core` owning the operator graph + checkpoint history. The
+  bus cannot drive it under the current `Action` trait (which
+  requires `&mut rge_kernel_ecs::World`). No current production user
+  path reaches it via a CommandBus submit either. Excluded as non-
+  eligible (the editor's CAD-graph mutations are out-of-band today —
+  see ISSUE-108 EXEC §Q3 R-list for the prior precedent).
+
+- egui inspector / dock UI mutations — **`editor-shell-field`** /
+  **`needs-new-context`**. `self.egui_host: Option<EguiHost>`
+  (`lifecycle/mod.rs:533`) and `self.inspector_handoff:
+  Option<Arc<InspectorHandoff>>` (`lifecycle/mod.rs:554`) are read by
+  `render_frame` only; there is no production back-channel from egui
+  widget events back to a CommandBus submit today. ISSUE-132 EXEC §Q2
+  R7 documents the same finding. Wiring such a back-channel is the
+  TASK packet's "UI/menu/toolbar/egui wiring" exclusion when paired
+  with time-scale; for non-time-scale targets it additionally requires
+  new context shape and is classified `needs-new-context`.
+
+**Summary of the world-only column.** Across every production
+non-time-scale user-visible candidate inventoried in Q1, **none**
+is classified `world-only` under the current `Action::apply(&mut
+rge_kernel_ecs::World)` contract. The only `world-only` impl in the
+workspace is `SetTimeScale`
+(`crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/commands.rs:193-264`), and that
+is the action whose follow-ups the TASK packet scope excludes.
+
+### Q3. Excluding time-scale, is there a smallest candidate that can use Approach A without widening CommandBus?
+
+**No candidate.**
+
+Approach A (per ISSUE-108 / ISSUE-132 and the TASK packet's
+Acceptance Criteria) is strictly: keep the existing
+`Action::apply(&self, &mut rge_kernel_ecs::World)` /
+`Action::revert(&self, &mut rge_kernel_ecs::World)` contract from
+`crates/editor-actions/src/action.rs:74-104`; no editor-state context
+threading, no adapter ledger, no Tier-2 promotion of
+`crates/editor-actions`, no widening of the `Action` trait, no
+change to `CommandBus::submit` / `undo` / `redo` signatures, no
+change to `CompoundAction`, no change to
+`EditorShell::submit_action`. Mutations must land on the editor
+wrapper world the bus actually sees (`self.world.kernel_mut()` —
+`commands.rs:280`).
+
+Excluding time-scale per TASK scope, every non-time-scale
+production user-visible candidate inventoried in Q1 classifies as
+`editor-shell-field`, `editor-state`, `cad/editor-wrapper-world`,
+`render/gfx`, or `needs-new-context` in Q2. Therefore none of them
+can be expressed as a `&mut rge_kernel_ecs::World` mutation under
+Approach A without either:
+
+- widening the `Action` trait (forbidden — `Action` trait widening
+  is explicitly excluded by Acceptance Criteria), OR
+- introducing an adapter that mutates editor-shell-field /
+  editor-state / cad-graph / render-gfx out-of-band (forbidden —
+  adapter ledger is explicitly excluded by Acceptance Criteria), OR
+- threading an editor-shell-side context into `Action` (forbidden —
+  editor-state context is explicitly excluded by Acceptance Criteria), OR
+- promoting `editor-actions` to a Tier-2 editor-domain coordinator
+  with a `cad-core` / `editor-state` dep (forbidden — Tier-2
+  promotion is explicitly excluded by Acceptance Criteria), OR
+- changing the `CommandBus::submit` signature to receive any context
+  other than `&mut rge_kernel_ecs::World` (forbidden — CommandBus
+  signature changes are explicitly excluded by Acceptance Criteria).
+
+Each of the above is a structural change to the bus/action surface,
+not a "smallest candidate". The only `world-only` mutation envelope
+in the current workspace is the `TimeScale` resource, and the TASK
+packet scope excludes every time-scale follow-up from being task #29
+(`SetTimeScale`, time-scale presets, step-up/step-down bindings,
+preset trio work, UI/menu/toolbar/egui wiring).
+
+No "invent a fresh `World` resource" path is available either:
+inventing a brand-new resource and a brand-new user action to mutate
+it would not be wiring an **existing** non-time-scale user action; it
+would be adding a new resource + new action + new keybinding +
+new UI + new test surface in a single dispatch, which is not "the
+smallest candidate". It would also pre-judge what editor state
+belongs in the wrapper kernel world vs `EditorCoord` vs `cad_world`
+vs `editor-shell-field` — that is the very architectural decision
+the TASK packet asks the human arbiter to make in Q4.
+
+### Q4. If a candidate exists, what is the smallest implementation surface and verification plan?
+
+No candidate exists under the strict Approach A reading. Per the TASK
+packet's Q4 directive ("If a candidate exists, what is the smallest
+implementation surface and verification plan? … If no such candidate
+exists, Q4 identifies the smallest human architecture decision needed
+before implementation can proceed"), the Executor names the smallest
+human architecture decision here:
+
+**The smallest human architecture decision needed before implementation
+can proceed is: *which* non-time-scale editor user action should
+become the next CommandBus-backed mutation, and *which* of its
+classified surfaces (`editor-shell-field`, `editor-state`,
+`cad/editor-wrapper-world`, `render/gfx`, `needs-new-context`)
+should grow a World-only representation so that Approach A can hold.**
+
+Concretely the human arbiter must choose one of the following lanes
+(this list is descriptive of the option space, not a recommendation;
+no source edit is proposed):
+
+- (Lane α — "mirror Selection into the wrapper world") Migrate the
+  `EditorCoord::selection: Selection` field (currently at
+  `crates/editor-shell/src/coord.rs:21`, owned by
+  `rge_editor_state::Selection` per `editor-state/src/selection.rs:58`)
+  into a typed `rge_kernel_ecs::World` resource or component on
+  `self.world.kernel`, so that a future "select entity" / "clear
+  selection" user action can submit a World-only `Action`. This would
+  require an `editor-state-ownership` architecture-lint exception or
+  doctrine change (per `editor-state/src/lib.rs:11-14`) — strictly a
+  human decision.
+- (Lane β — "mirror PlayState into the wrapper world") Migrate the
+  `EditorShell::state: PlayState` field
+  (`crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs:249`) into the wrapper
+  world as a `PlayState`-typed resource. Playback transitions become
+  candidate World-only actions. Would conflict with
+  `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/playback.rs:6` ("No CommandBus
+  involvement; no new ECS state") which is currently the locked
+  design; reversing it requires human sign-off.
+- (Lane γ — "freeze the bus at time-scale until a real ECS-resident
+  user action lands") Accept that no non-time-scale World-only user
+  action exists today and defer further CommandBus wiring until a
+  future dispatch lands a `rge_kernel_ecs::World`-resident state with
+  a natural user-action shape (e.g. a future "ambient gravity"
+  resource, a future "tick budget" resource, or whatever the gameplay
+  surface introduces). The bus stays usable for tests and the
+  existing `Ctrl+2` / `Ctrl+0` / `Ctrl+4` lane; no source change is
+  needed today.
+- (Lane δ — "explicitly relax Approach A") Acknowledge that the only
+  meaningful next CommandBus user actions require editor-state
+  context, cad-graph context, or both, and revisit ISSUE-108's
+  Approach B/C decision with the latest evidence. Out of scope for
+  this read-only audit per the TASK packet's exclusion of "Approach
+  B, Approach C, `Action` trait widening, adapter ledger, editor-
+  state context, CommandBus signature changes".
+
+This selection is **not** something the Executor can make from
+read-only code evidence: it is a doctrine / architecture decision
+that affects `crates/editor-state` ownership rules
+(`editor-state/src/lib.rs:11-19`), `crates/editor-shell` field
+ownership, and the workspace's freeze-policy register.
+
+### Q5. What should task #29 be?
+
+**`NEEDS_HUMAN`.**
+
+Per the TASK packet Acceptance Criteria: "If no non-time-scale
+World-only candidate exists under the current `Action` trait, Q5
+must end with `NEEDS_HUMAN`; the EXEC report must use
+`STATUS: NEEDS_HUMAN`, include `EXEC_STATUS: blocked`, and end with
+footer `HANDOFF_STATUS: NEEDS_HUMAN`, `NEXT_ROLE: HUMAN_ARBITER`,
+`EXIT_CODE: 0`."
+
+This dispatch's audit confirms — with file/line evidence in Q1 and
+Q2 — that no current non-time-scale production editor user action
+mutates only `&mut rge_kernel_ecs::World` on the editor wrapper world
+the bus sees. The only `world-only` impl in the workspace is
+`SetTimeScale`, and the TASK packet's scope excludes every time-scale
+follow-up from being task #29.
+
+The human arbiter should pick one of the four lanes described in Q4
+(α — migrate Selection; β — migrate PlayState; γ — freeze the bus
+at time-scale; δ — relax Approach A) before any further CommandBus
+implementation work is scheduled. The Executor does NOT recommend
+"another time-scale preset" or "step-up/step-down" or "preset trio"
+or "UI/menu/toolbar/egui wiring of time-scale" — all of those are
+forbidden by the TASK packet Acceptance Criteria.
+
+`NEEDS_HUMAN`.
+
+## Deviations from Task Packet
+
+None — execution stayed strictly within the Task Packet scope. The
+audit was read-only; only this single `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-140_EXEC_*.md`
+report was created. No source, test, Cargo, workflow, script,
+doctrine, status, planning, ADR, fixture, generated asset, existing
+handoff packet, dispatch log, GitHub issue, label, PR, branch,
+review, comment, commit, remote state, or queue-runner metadata was
+edited. No correction round was invoked. No cargo, formatter,
+architecture-lint, test, doctest, bench, source-generation,
+docs-generation, or `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` command was run, per
+the TASK packet's `Verification Gates` ("No cargo, formatter,
+architecture-lint, or test command is required as an Executor gate
+for this read-only audit").
+
+## Open Questions for Reviewer
+
+- The Q4 lane list (α / β / γ / δ) is enumerative, not normative.
+  The Executor classified each candidate strictly by current code
+  evidence; the arbitration between the lanes (which editor-state /
+  editor-shell-field axis, if any, should grow a World-only
+  representation, and on what timeline) is the human decision the
+  TASK packet expects. The Executor did not inspect
+  `crates/tool-architecture-lints/**` or workspace-level ADRs /
+  PLAN.md sections covering the
+  `editor-state-ownership`-lint and the `editor-actions` tier
+  classification because those are outside the TASK packet's
+  mandatory audit roots; if any of them already pre-resolves Lanes
+  α/β/γ/δ, the arbiter may be able to short-circuit the decision
+  without further dispatch overhead.
+- The `EditorCoord::face_selection` writer in
+  `pick_path.rs:94-97` is the closest analogue to a "real user
+  action that the bus could plausibly mediate", but it lands in
+  `editor-state` (and rebuilds a `render/gfx` index buffer) per Q2,
+  not on the wrapper world. The classification is reported here
+  verbatim from the code; the arbiter may want to weigh face-pick
+  as the lane α exemplar specifically (mirror `FaceSelection` into
+  the wrapper world as a typed component / resource), or wait until
+  a non-face-pick editor action surfaces.
+- The `cad_world: Option<KernelWorld>` field
+  (`lifecycle/mod.rs:301`) is a distinct `rge_kernel_ecs::World`
+  from the bus-mediated `self.world.kernel`. The current bus
+  contract cannot reach `cad_world` even though both are
+  `rge_kernel_ecs::World` instances. If the arbiter wants the
+  CommandBus to mediate CAD-domain state, that requires more than a
+  "smallest" implementation dispatch — it requires deciding which
+  of the two kernel worlds the bus owns, which is a doctrine-level
+  question beyond this audit.
+

+

+## Goal
+
+Perform GitHub issue #140 as a read-only preflight for the next non-time-scale, World-only CommandBus action. Tasks #25, #26, and #27 proved the CommandBus path through `SetTimeScale`; this audit must inspect the current editor-shell command, playback, reload, editor-actions, editor-state, test, and prior EXEC packet evidence to decide whether there is now a smallest non-time-scale user action that fits Approach A under the existing `Action::apply(&mut rge_kernel_ecs::World)` trait. The result is a single EXEC report that answers Q1-Q5 and recommends task #29 or routes to `NEEDS_HUMAN`.
+
+## Scope
+
+### MAY edit
+- None. This is an audit-only dispatch.
+
+### MUST NOT edit
+- Any `crates/**` source or test file.
+- Any `editor/**` file.
+- Any `kernel/**` file.
+- Any `.github/**` workflow file.
+- Any Cargo file, including `Cargo.toml`, `Cargo.lock`, workspace manifests, generated Cargo metadata, or dependency declarations.
+- Any PowerShell script, shell script, schema, architecture lint, doctrine document, status document, planning document, ADR, fixture, generated asset, or root-level repository document.
+- Any existing handoff packet or existing dispatch log, including this TASK packet after execution begins.
+- Any GitHub issue, label, PR, branch, review, comment, commit, remote state, or queue metadata except the queue runner's normal issue lifecycle for this dispatch.
+
+### MAY add new files
+- Exactly one execution report matching `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-140_EXEC_*.md`.
+- The matching `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-140_EXEC_*.meta.json` sidecar only if `new-handoff.ps1 -Finalize` creates it while finalizing the EXEC report.
+- This dispatch's own queue-runner log under `ai_dispatch_logs/log_*.md` only if the queue runner creates it as part of normal dispatch operation.
+
+### MUST NOT add new files
+- New source files, test files, fixtures, docs, generated assets, Cargo files, dependency files, schemas, scripts, workflows, architecture lints, TASK packets, REVIEW packets, CLOSEOUT packets, issue notes, or design documents.
+- Any handoff packet other than this dispatch's single `ISSUE-140_EXEC_*.md` report and optional matching `.meta.json` sidecar.
+
+## Deliverables
+
+- One read-only `EXECUTION_REPORT` packet for ISSUE-140.
+- The EXEC report contains a literal `## 5-Question Non-Time-Scale CommandBus Preflight Answer Block` section.
+- Under that section, the EXEC report contains these exact Q1-Q5 heading lines:
+  - `### Q1. What non-time-scale editor user actions exist today, and where are they handled?`
+  - `### Q2. Which candidates are genuinely World-only under the current Action trait?`
+  - `### Q3. Excluding time-scale, is there a smallest candidate that can use Approach A without widening CommandBus?`
+  - `### Q4. If a candidate exists, what is the smallest implementation surface and verification plan?`
+  - `### Q5. What should task #29 be?`
+- Q1 inventories keyboard, toolbar, reload, selection, playback, tool, and other obvious non-time-scale editor-shell user actions with file/line evidence.
+- Q2 classifies every candidate as exactly one of `world-only`, `editor-shell-field`, `editor-state`, `cad/editor-wrapper-world`, `render/gfx`, or `needs-new-context`, with file/line evidence for each classification.
+- Q3 applies Approach A strictly and excludes every time-scale follow-up, including `SetTimeScale`, time-scale presets, step-up/step-down bindings, preset trio work, UI/menu/toolbar/egui wiring, and any other time-scale task.
+- Q4 names an implementation surface and verification plan only if Q3 finds a true non-time-scale World-only candidate under the current `Action` trait. If no such candidate exists, Q4 identifies the smallest human architecture decision needed before implementation can proceed.
+- Q5 recommends exactly one of: a bounded implementation task for the chosen non-time-scale World-only candidate, a narrower follow-up read-only audit, or `NEEDS_HUMAN` because no eligible candidate exists under the current Action trait.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- The dispatch is read-only except for its own generated EXEC report, optional matching sidecar, and possible queue-runner log.
+- The EXEC report includes the exact five-question block title and exact Q1-Q5 heading lines listed in Deliverables.
+- Every candidate classification is tied to current file/line evidence, not desired architecture or assumptions from file names.
+- `SetTimeScale`, Ctrl+2, Ctrl+0, Ctrl+4, future time-scale presets, time-scale step-up/step-down bindings, preset trio work, UI/menu/toolbar/egui wiring, and any other time-scale follow-up are excluded from the recommended task #29.
+- Approach A is the only permitted architecture: current `Action::apply(&mut rge_kernel_ecs::World)` / `Action::revert(&mut rge_kernel_ecs::World)`, no editor-state context, no adapter ledger, no Tier-2 promotion, no `Action` trait widening, and no `CommandBus` signature change.
+- If no non-time-scale World-only candidate exists under the current `Action` trait, Q5 must end with `NEEDS_HUMAN`; the EXEC report must use `STATUS: NEEDS_HUMAN`, include `EXEC_STATUS: blocked`, and end with footer `HANDOFF_STATUS: NEEDS_HUMAN`, `NEXT_ROLE: HUMAN_ARBITER`, `EXIT_CODE: 0`.
+- If a candidate exists, Q5 must name one bounded task #29 route with exact candidate, exact allowed files, exact tests, exact MUST NOT list, focused gates, canonical gates if relevant, and halt conditions. The EXEC report then uses normal successful EXEC routing to the reviewer.
+- The final worktree check confirms no tracked source, test, Cargo, workflow, script, doctrine, status-doc, existing-packet, issue-label, or other forbidden edit occurred.
+
+## Constraints / Non-Goals
+
+- Do not implement the chosen candidate in this dispatch.
+- Do not edit tests or add test coverage in this dispatch.
+- Do not run a correction round that fixes source, tests, Cargo, workflows, scripts, doctrine, status docs, issues, labels, or existing packets.
+- Do not propose Approach B, Approach C, `Action` trait widening, adapter ledger, editor-state context, CommandBus signature changes, `CompoundAction` changes, or any other alternative architecture.
+- Do not use another time-scale action as the task #29 recommendation.
+- Do not infer dispatch completion from missing `_REVIEW_` or `_CLOSEOUT_` packets; this orchestrator dispatch normally writes TASK and EXEC packets only.
+- Do not commit, push, open a PR, create or edit GitHub issues, change labels, or write follow-up packets.
+
+## Verification Gates
+
+The Executor MUST run and document the result of each of these in the `EXECUTION_REPORT`:
+
+- `git status --short --untracked-files=no` before any audit work. Document all pre-existing tracked changes if any are visible. Do not clean, revert, or modify unrelated user changes.
+- `git status --short --untracked-files=no` after writing the EXEC report. Confirm that visible tracked changes, if any, are limited to this dispatch's own `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-140_*` packet artifacts and/or this dispatch's own `ai_dispatch_logs/log_*.md` artifact, and that no tracked source, test, Cargo, workflow, script, doctrine, status-doc, existing-packet, or issue/label metadata file changed.
+
+No cargo, formatter, architecture-lint, or test command is required as an Executor gate for this read-only audit. If the orchestrator or queue runner runs repository verification after EXEC and that verification fails outside this audit's allowed artifact surface, do not fix source or tests; halt/report according to the halt conditions below.
+
+## Halt Conditions
+
+The Executor MUST halt without broadening scope and request fresh guidance if any of the following occur:
+
+- Answering Q1-Q5 requires editing source, tests, Cargo, workflows, scripts, doctrine, status docs, issues, labels, existing packets, or any other forbidden file.
+- The current code cannot be inspected enough to provide file/line evidence for the required candidate classifications.
+- The only possible recommendation requires changing `rge_editor_actions::Action`, `CommandBus`, `CompoundAction`, `EditorShell::submit_action`, `EditorShell` context shape, editor-state APIs, or any CommandBus signature.
+- The smallest apparent candidate requires editor-state mutation, editor-shell field mutation, render/gfx mutation, CAD wrapper-world mutation, asset reload side effects, playback state mutation, UI/menu/toolbar/egui wiring, or broader editor context that does not fit the current World-only `Action` trait.
+- The answer tries to pick `SetTimeScale`, another time-scale preset, step-up/step-down binding, preset trio work, UI/menu/toolbar/egui wiring, or any other time-scale follow-up as task #29.
+- Prior CommandBus EXEC packet evidence needed for Approach A or the landed time-scale precedent is missing, unreadable, or contradictory enough that candidate classification cannot be trusted.
+- A required verification command shows tracked changes outside this dispatch's own allowed handoff/log artifacts.
+- Any forbidden edit or forbidden artifact is created. Stop immediately, do not expand the diff, and report the deviation in the EXEC report.
+- A correction prompt asks the Executor to fix any source/test/Cargo/workflow/script/doc/status failure for this read-only audit. Do not execute that correction; write or update the allowed EXEC report with `STATUS: NEEDS_HUMAN`, `EXEC_STATUS: blocked`, footer `HANDOFF_STATUS: NEEDS_HUMAN`, `NEXT_ROLE: HUMAN_ARBITER`, and `EXIT_CODE: 0`.
+
+If Q1-Q5 are answerable but no eligible non-time-scale World-only candidate exists, complete the five-question block and route the EXEC report to `NEEDS_HUMAN` as specified in Acceptance Criteria. Do not invent a task #29 implementation surface.

+## Planner Notes
+
+This dispatch comes directly from `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md` task #28 and GitHub issue #140. Task #24 / ISSUE-132 selected Approach A after the earlier ISSUE-108 CommandBus audit; tasks #25, #26, and #27 then implemented Ctrl+2, Ctrl+0, and Ctrl+4 by reusing the existing `SetTimeScale` action. This audit must move past that time-scale lane and verify whether any non-time-scale editor action already fits the same World-only contract.
+
+Use prior EXEC packets as evidence, not authority to skip current inspection. The code may have changed since those packets were written, and Q1-Q5 must cite the current files and lines. The outcome is not pre-decided: a real non-time-scale World-only candidate may become task #29, but if none exists under the current Action trait, the correct result is `NEEDS_HUMAN`.

+MUST classify each candidate as world-only, editor-shell-field, editor-state, cad/editor-wrapper-world, render/gfx, or needs-new-context using file/line evidence
+MUST exclude SetTimeScale, time-scale presets, step-up/step-down bindings, preset trio work, UI/menu/toolbar/egui wiring, and any other time-scale follow-up from the recommended task #29
+MUST apply Approach A strictly; do not propose Approach B, Approach C, Action trait widening, adapter ledger, editor-state context, or CommandBus signature changes as alternatives
+MUST end with NEEDS_HUMAN if no non-time-scale World-only candidate exists under the current Action trait rather than inventing an implementation task
+MUST run git status --short --untracked-files=no before and after EXEC and confirm only this dispatch's own ai_handoffs/log artifacts changed
